channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: ICE looking to expand migrant detention facilities across country, ACLU says
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union says information it has received from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement indicates that several migrant detention facilities on the South Texas border are among areas where the agency are seeking information to possibly expand under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lebanon's parliament elects army commander Joseph Aoun as president, ending a 2-year deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s parliament voted Thursday to elect army commander Joseph Aoun as head of state, filling a more than two-year-long presidential vacuum.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wildfires latest: Winds subside as firefighters battle devastating blazes in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters are battling to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed five people, ravaged communities from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter to receive state funeral in DC before Georgia burial
item.description: President Joe Biden, who was the first sitting senator to endorse Carter's 1976 presidential campaign, will deliver a eulogy.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, KREX 5

item.title: Indiana man killed girlfriend's 4-month-old puppy: Docs
item.description: An Indiana man faces a felony charge after police say he threw his girlfriend's dog onto the ground in frustration, killing the 4-month-old puppy.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KRON4

item.title: Chemical used for explosives found in New Orleans terrorist's storage unit: FBI
item.description: FBI Houston says bottles of sulfuric acid were found inside a cooler at a Harris County storage facility rented by the Bourbon Street terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Convicted murderer Alex Murdaugh ordered to pay $14.8M for insurance scheme
item.description: A judge ordered Murdaugh to pay Nautilus Insurance $14.8 million for an insurance scheme involving the family of his former housekeeper.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Davis, NewsNation

item.title: The polar vortex readies to dump snow on Texas and its neighbors
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — An area stretching from Texas to Tennessee braced Wednesday for the expected arrival of freezing rain and snow, as some other parts of the country that already received an arctic blast this week prepared to go another round with the plunging polar vortex.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A new book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team an early look at town hall questions
item.description: A forthcoming book alleges that someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump’s presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jingle! contract workers say they're still waiting to get paid
item.description: Joe and Nikol Straws, two popular DJs from Wyandotte County, performed at Jingle! in Kansas City, Kansas, and said they're owed $18,600.
item.dc:creator: Sean McDowell, NewsNation

item.title: Santos seeks to delay sentencing, hoping to pay off fines with podcast
item.description: Former Rep. George Santos (R-N.Y.) is looking to delay sentencing on federal fraud charges so he can pay off his fines through funds generated by his new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Military doctor pleads guilty to dozens of sexual abuse charges
item.description: A military doctor has pleaded guilty to sexually abusing dozens of soldiers at Joint Base Lewis-McChord who had primarily sought his care for pain management. Maj.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: High-profile real estate brother misses hearing after mix-up in identical twins' sex trafficking case
item.description: One of three wealthy brothers accused of drugging and raping dozens of women in multiple states reportedly missed his court hearing on Tuesday after a mix-up in the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio's COVID-era health director becomes first Dem to file for governor
item.description: “I’m a doctor, not a politician,” she said in a statement announcing her candidacy. “I solve problems by bringing people together regardless of party to find solutions.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Four San Diego State fraternity members charged after one set on fire: DA
item.description: The incident involving the Phi Kappa Psi members occurred last February, when the fraternity was on probation with the university due to issues of hazing .
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Hollywood Hills blaze adds new danger to devastating LA fires
item.description: Combined, the fires have consumed about 22 square miles — about half the size of the entire city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, Fox 5

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: President Joe Biden said early Wednesday that he thinks he would have beaten President-elect Donald Trump if he stuck with his reelection bid in an interview with USA Today.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Colleges prepare for new legal and political terrain under Trump
item.description: Colleges and universities are preparing for new legal and political territory as President-elect Trump returns to office. Concerns for international students, GOP attacks on higher education and increased protests on campus are just some of the worries on administrators' minds.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fierce overnight winds expected to fuel Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: “We looked across and the fire had jumped from one side of the road to the other side of the road,” Resident Kelsey Trainor said. “People were getting out of the cars with their dogs and babies and bags, they were crying and screaming. The road was just blocked, like full-on blocked for an hour.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans is a city marked by tragedy. But don’t call locals resilient
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — She ran around in silver sparkling shoes, her faux chainmail tunic shimmering in the freezing breeze, maneuvering horses made of paper mâché, a giant green dragon, and sheep constructed from milk cartons.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, SHARON LURYE, CLAIRE GALOFARO and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: PALM BEACH, Florida (AP) — Less than two weeks before taking office, President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday took some of his most audacious claims and promises of the transition period and amped them up to new levels.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, AAMER MADHANI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3-year-old boy dies from Honolulu fireworks explosion injuries, raising toll to 4
item.description: The U.S. military flew six of the injured to Phoenix for treatment on Saturday because Hawaii’s lone burn unit doesn’t have enough capacity to care for all the victims.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Meta replaces fact-checking with X-style community notes
item.description: Facebook and Instagram owner Meta said Tuesday it's scrapping its third-party fact checking program and replacing it with a Community Notes program written by users similar to the model used by Elon Musk's social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Strong earthquake kills at least 95 people in western China near Mount Everest
item.description: A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble, and killing at least 95 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: First US bird flu death is announced in Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first U.S. bird flu death has been reported — a person in Louisiana who had been hospitalized with severe respiratory symptoms.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After 2 years of shortfalls, California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit
item.description: TURLOCK, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's launching new value menu: Here are the options
item.description: McDonald’s will launch a new “McValue platform” on Tuesday, aiming to give customers “even more ways to enjoy the food they love, for less," the fast-food giant says. The McValue menu, first announced in November, will include nationwide, local and app-based offers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Border agents in California find child allegedly abducted from Texas
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection said agents recovered a missing child last week and apprehended a driver wanted for their alleged abduction.
item.dc:creator: TMX, KTLA 5

item.title: Flight cancellations pile up as major winter storm hits East Coast
item.description: Flight cancellations and delays are stacking up as a major winter storm makes its way across the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: DOJ weighs 200 outstanding Capitol riot cases referred by FBI
item.description: Federal prosecutors are mulling bringing charges against as many as 200 more people for their roles in the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, including some 60 individuals suspected of assaulting or impeding law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
